title: POLICIES AND PROCEDURES
intro: A number of policies/procedures are in place to guide students during their studentship in the University.

sections.title: Procedure for Registration of Fresh Students for Academic Activities
sections.content: All fresh students of the University must go through the registration process through the completion of relevant documents. Registration of fresh students will start from the Admissions Office of the University and end in the Departments. The procedure for the registration of students is as follows:

steps.content: All fresh students shall obtain their admission letters from the Admissions Unit in the Academic Affairs Division of the University.
All fresh students shall present the following documents to the registration officers at the clearance centre:
list: i. Letter of Provisional Admission into Federal University of Technology, Ikot Abasi (FUTIA) issued by JAMB;
ii. Evidence of payment of Acceptance Fee;
iii. Academic credentials (certificates) and JAMB Result slips;
iv. Birth certificates or Statutory Declaration of Age;
v. Certificate of Origin from the Local Government of the student;
vi. Letter of Attestation from student's parents/guardians/clergy vouching for his/her good behaviour and conduct;
vii. Certificate of medical fitness from FUTIA Medical Unit;
viii. Five (5) recent coloured passport-size photographs with the student's name and course of study clearly written on the reverse side of photographs; and,
ix. Five (5) photocopies of each item i-vi above.

steps.content: All fresh students shall present all the items in Step 2 above to the Clearance Officer at the Clearance Centre.

After the Clearance Officer had certified that the student meets the general and course entry requirements, he/she shall pay the appropriate fee in any of the banks using a generated Remita Retrieval Reference (RRR) Number and obtain a bank receipt.
All fresh students shall submit the bank receipt to Students Accounts Unit in the University Bursary, and obtain a University Bursary Receipt.
All fresh students shall present at the Clearance Centre the original and five(5) photocopies of the University Bursary Receipt and obtain the following forms from the Clearance Officer:
list: i. Acceptance Form: (FUTIA/ACAD/01)
ii. Certificate of Clearance (FUTIA/ACAD/02)
iii. General Registration Form (FUTIA/ACAD/03)
iv. Statutory Declaration of good behaviour (FUTIA/ACAD/04)
v. Federal University of Technology, Ikot Abasi Admissions Letter (FUTIA/ACAD/05)
vi. The students will be given FUTIA Matriculation number.
a. Complete all the listed forms mentioned in step 6;
b. Take the completed Statutory Declaration Form of good behaviour to the High Court, for attestation by the Commissioner for Oaths;
c. Make five (5) photocopies of the completed Acceptance Form, Certificate of Clearance, General Registration and Statutory Declaration Forms and submit them to the Clearance Officer.
steps.additional_info: The Clearance Officer shall sign the Certificate of Clearance after all the required documents have been completed and submitted.

steps.content: All students shall proceed to the irrespective College Secretaries for Course Registration forms with two sets of these documents.

At the Departmental Office, students shall be provided with the list of courses for First and Second Semesters. He/she shall enter the appropriate courses in the First and Second Semester Course Registration Forms and submit them at his/her Departmental Office.

steps.title: PROCEDURE TO OBTAIN STUDENT'S IDENTITY (ID) CARD
steps.content: When all these processes are complete, the student is expected to proceed to the ICT unit to process and obtain an Identity Card.
note: It is only when the Student Course Registration Forms have been completed and duly signed by appropriate Officers and the student's particulars entered in the student list format that registration would be deemed to have been duly completed.

Students who do not complete all the nine (9) steps mentioned above would not be issued Matriculation Numbers. A student without a Matriculation Number is not deemed to be a bona fide student of the Federal University of Technology Ikot Abasi and would not be allowed to take lectures, write examinations and cannot be issued a Student's I.D. Card. It is important to emphasize that the clearance and registration exercises last for only three (3) weeks.

steps.title: REPLACEMENT OF LOST STUDENT'S IDENTITY (ID) CARD
steps.content: A lost ID card will be replaced only upon production of a police report/court affidavit, a letter of introduction from the Director and payment of the prescribed fee as replacement charge. Where the ID card expires, the student should return same to the University for Re-issuance.
steps.note: It is the responsibility of the student to ensure he/she replaces his/her ID card when lost or expired.

sections.title: Registration of Returning Students
sections.content: All returning students of the University are required to register for the prescribed courses in their programmes of study every Academic Session. Students are expected to complete their registration within two (2) weeks from the date of commencement of the First Semester in the Session.

sections.title: Late Registration
sections.content: The University allows two (2) weeks after normal registration for late registration. Late registration attracts a charge. The charge is subject to review from time to time by the University Management.

sections.title: Registration at other Units
sections.intro: All students are required to register upon presentation of evidence of payment of school fees at the following units every Academic Session:
list: i. Health Centre
ii. University Library
iii. Student Affairs Division
iv. Student Hostels
v. Centre for Entrepreneurial Development.

subsections.subtitle: 2.5.1 Health Centre
subsections.content: Every student is required to register with and undergo medical examination at the University Health Centre.

subsections.subtitle: 2.5.2 University Library
subsections.content: All students are required to register with the University library and renew same every session upon the presentation of evidence of payment of school fees.

subsections.subtitle: 2.5.3 Student Affairs Division
subsections.content: Students should make all enquiries at the Student Affairs Division and ensure that they participate in all approved University activities, namely: Orientation, Matriculation, Lectures, Seminars, Workshops, Continuous Assessments, Practicals and Examinations etc. It is mandatory that every student obtains a copy of the Student Handbook.

subsections.subtitle: 2.5.4 Student Hostels
subsections.content: Students entitled to accommodation shall proceed to the Student Affairs Division for necessary Hostel registration.

subsections.subtitle: 2.5.5 Centre for Entrepreneurial Development
subsections.content: Every student is required to register with the Centre for Entrepreneurial Development.

sections.title: Certificate Verification
sections.content: There shall be verification of certificates and credentials submitted to the University during the period of studentship in the Federal University of Technology Ikot Abasi. Students found to have submitted falsified certificates and credentials shall be expelled from the University and may be handed over to the Law Enforcement Agencies. The University reserves the right to withdraw any degree/diploma/certificate of any student whose admission into the University is discovered to have been obtained through the presentation of false information at the time of admission.

sections.title: Change of Name
sections.content: A male or female student is allowed to change the name with which he/she gained admission into the Federal University of Technology Ikot Abasi, with legal evidence including newspaper publication.
sections.note: The process of change of name is only effective from 400 level with a formal letter informing the University, addressed to the Registrar through his/her Head of Department, with legal evidence including newspaper publication.
